FastLayout Engine v1.1

C++ vs JavaScript
at scale.

A flexbox layout engine compiled from C++ to WebAssembly, benchmarked live in your browser against pure JavaScript — same algorithm, same data, no shortcuts.

50,000
C++ / WASM
—
milliseconds avg
FASTER
Pure JavaScript
—
milliseconds avg
FASTER
SPEEDUP FACTOR
— ×
ℹ heap-safe call: Input JSON is written directly to WASM heap memory via _malloc + stringToUTF8 — bypassing cwrap's stack allocation, which was the cause of the OOB crash on large payloads.
// FastLayout benchmark ready.
// Using heap-safe WASM calls — no stack overflow at any node count.
// C++ wins above ~3–5k nodes where compute dominates over JSON overhead.
Why JS sometimes wins at low counts: The JS baseline mutates plain objects with zero serialisation cost. Every WASM call pays a fixed ~0.5–1 ms overhead: JSON encode → heap copy → WASM parse → solve → serialise → JS decode. That overhead is only beaten when the C++ solver's speed advantage on actual computation is large enough — which happens around 3–5k nodes and grows sharply beyond that. The OOB crash was caused by cwrap writing the JSON input onto the WASM stack via stringToUTF8OnStack. A 5k-node tree produces ~500 KB of JSON — far exceeding any stack budget. The fix uses _malloc to put it on the heap instead.
